title: Preparation of NC64A “Ghosts”
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"84t3p","text":"MATERIALS:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":10}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4ud45","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4e84u","text":" 1) NC64A chlorella, pelleted (fresh cells preferred)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":10,"length":9}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"99coi","text":" 2) 70% methanol","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"2lbgk","text":" 3) Centrifuge tubes, corex or polypropylene","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"ojr0","text":" 4) 50 mM Tris-HCl, pH 7.8","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Centrifuge the NC64A chlorella in the Sorvall centrifuge at 5,000 rpm, 5 min, 4°C if fresh cells are used. 00:05:00
If frozen cells are used, thaw the pellets and suspend with 50 mM Tris-HCl, pH 7.8. Centrifuge the cells in the Sorvall SS34 rotor or HB-4 rotor at 5,000 rpm, 5 min, 4°C. 00:05:00
Centrifuge the cells in the Sorvall SS34 rotor or HB-4 rotor at 5,000 rpm, 5 min, 4°C. 00:05:00
Repeat the 70% methanol washes 5-6 times or until the desired amount of pigment has been removed from the cells.
Store the chlorella “ghosts” at 4°C in 70% methanol.
Add 10-15 mL of 70% methanol to the chlorella pellets and resuspend the cells completely.
creator: Irina Agarkova
